title: Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of West Bengal

heroImage.alt: Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary, West Bengal

overview.short: Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Darjeeling District, West Bengal.
overview.description: Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Darjeeling District, West Bengal. Situated at an elevation of about 2 km, it is the habitat of some high-altitude animals like Himalayan Salamander (Tylototriton verrucosus), locally known as 'Gora'. Many animals and birds are found there. Jore Pokhari is a small village on a hilltop which is almost 20 kilometers away from Darjeeling town.

topPlaces.name: Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Sukhia Pokhri
topPlaces.description: Sukhia Pokhri is a census town in the Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri CD block in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in the indian state of West Bengal. It is located 11 km from Ghum on the way to Mirik.

topPlaces.name: Poobong Tea Garden
topPlaces.description: Poobong Tea Garden (also spelled Pubong) is a village in the Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri CD block in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Chamong Tea Garden
topPlaces.description: Chamong Tea Garden (also called Chamu Tea Garden) is a village in the Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri CD block in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Pokhriabong
topPlaces.description: Pokhriabong (also spelt as Pokhribong or Pokhrabong) is a village in the Jorebunglow Sukhiapokri CD block in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Manebhanjyang, Darjeeling
topPlaces.description: Maney Bhanjyang (1,946 m) is a small transit-town in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision, Darjeeling district in the state of West Bengal in India. It is located at the gateway of the Singalila National Park, 28 km away from Darjeeling.

topPlaces.name: Pokhriabong Lepcha Monastery
topPlaces.description: Pokhriabong Lepcha Monastery, also popularly known as the "Boudha Terda Pema Lingpa Lepcha Community Gompa", is located in the Indian state of West Bengal, approximately 30 km from the town of Darjeeling at a place called Pokhriabong. The monastery follows the teachings and practices of the Nyingma school, which is the oldest of the four major schools of Tibetan Buddhism, founded by the Vajrayana revealer Guru Padmasambhava.

topPlaces.name: Tumsong Tea Garden
topPlaces.description: Tumsong Tea Garden is a village in the Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri CD block in the Darjeeling Sadar subdivision of the Darjeeling district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (38 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jore Pokhri Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jorebunglow Sukhiapokhri (community development block), Sukhia Pokhri, Poobong Tea Garden, Chamong Tea Garden, Pokhriabong.
